generated_comment: Arre bhai, if she knew he was married then why get so close in the first place? Aadmi kabhi kabhi weak pad jaate hain, but aisi ladkiyan hi ghar todti hain, bilkul serial wali baat hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it excuses the man's infidelity as weakness while assigning primary blame to the woman for 'breaking' the family. It encodes blame asymmetry, homewrecker framing, and the 'she knew he was married' seductress narrative.
original_frame: Women's Opinions Dismissed: She Doesn't Know Enough to Have a Say
persona_text: You are Rohit. Age 29. Single. A man in Pune. You are at a family WhatsApp group discussion Ñ the group has 18 members across three generations. Your aunt has posted a long and detailed message about a financial decision the family needs to make regarding a shared investment. Her message is well researched and specific. Within five minutes two male cousins have replied dismissing her concern as overthinking. Your uncle has posted saying he will handle it. Your aunt has not replied. You are watching this happen on your phone. You add a comment. What you add reveals whether you are going to be part of the dismissal or whether you are going to do something different.
